key_insight: PyroDash teaches a small language model to decide token-by-token when to hand off to a frozen large model, reducing LLM usage while keeping strong mathematical reasoning performance.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical, router-free inference design for lowering serving cost in reasoning systems by moving the collaboration policy into the SLM itself.
method: Three-stage SLM training: control-token embedding learning, offloading-oriented supervised fine-tuning, and cost-aware alignment with GRPO.
At generation time, the SLM emits a control token to trigger a single handoff to a frozen LLM with the partial reasoning trace.
Reward optimization balances answer accuracy against normalized inference cost, without requiring LLM logits or LLM retraining.
evidence_signal: Across five math reasoning benchmarks, PyroDash achieves different accuracy-cost tradeoffs; at λ=0.05 it reaches 64.04% average accuracy with 20.4% lower cost, and at λ=0.6 it cuts total cost from USD 49.36 to USD 1.78 with only 1.90% LLM token ratio.
watch_item: Performance depends on learning a reliable control policy inside the SLM, so transfer to other domains or prompt styles may require retraining and careful cost-accuracy tuning.
paper_title: PyroDash: Cost-Efficient Token-Level Small-Large Language Model Collaborative Inference
